name: Bar-Compliance Guardrail Layer
description: Reusable sub-workflow that wraps any client-facing automation with ABA Op. 512-aligned compliance: opt-out list check, TCPA disclaimer insertion, and audit logging. Call it from every template that sends a client message. Returns { approved, message_out, suppression_reason } — the calling workflow sends only if approved is true and uses message_out (which contains the disclaimer). One Google Sheet, two tabs: Opt-Outs and Audit Log. Also includes a Twilio STOP handler webhook (Path B) that automatically writes STOP replies to the Opt-Outs tab and removes UNSTOP replies — activate the workflow to enable it.

parameters.content:
## Bar-Compliance Guardrail

**Activate this workflow** after wiring credentials — the Twilio STOP handler (Path B) requires it active to receive inbound SMS. The sub-workflow path (Path A) is called by parent templates and works whether or not the workflow is active.

**Path A — Sub-workflow (compliance check before every send)**
Parent templates call this via their 'Call Compliance Guardrail' node before every client message.

**What Path A does per call:**
1. Receives the planned message + recipient from the calling template
2. Checks your Google Sheet opt-out list — suppresses opted-out contacts automatically
3. Appends the required TCPA opt-out instruction (SMS: 'Reply STOP to opt out.' / email: unsubscribe footer)
4. Logs every attempt (approved or suppressed) to your audit log
5. Returns all caller fields plus `{ approved, message_out, suppression_reason }` to the caller

**Path B — Twilio STOP handler (inbound SMS)**
Receives STOP and UNSTOP replies directly from clients and updates the Opt-Outs tab in real time:
- STOP / UNSUBSCRIBE / CANCEL / END / QUIT → appends the sender to Opt-Outs (deduplication built in)
- UNSTOP / START → removes the sender from Opt-Outs so future messages are re-enabled
- Any other inbound SMS → silently skipped (HTTP 200 returned to Twilio)

**Path B setup:**
① Activate this workflow (toggle top-right after wiring credentials)
② Twilio Console → Phone Numbers → your number → Messaging Configuration → 'When a message comes in' → paste the Production URL from 'Receive STOP Reply' (path: twilio-stop) → HTTP POST → Save

⚠️ One inbound URL per Twilio number: if you run NTC-17 (Review Request Engine) on the same Twilio number, pointing it here will stop NTC-17 from receiving client rating replies. Use a separate Twilio number for review requests, or consolidate inbound routing into one entry-point workflow.

**Replaces:** No off-the-shelf equivalent — this is the ABA Op. 512 operational-compliance layer made reusable.

**Satisfies:**
- TCPA: opt-out instruction on every outbound SMS; real-time STOP capture
- ABA Op. 512: operational automation with no legal judgment
- Firm risk: catch opted-out contacts before a message is ever sent

**Storage: one Google Sheet, two tabs**
- `Opt-Outs` columns: phone, email, opted_out_at, source
- `Audit Log` columns: logged_at, template_id, matter_id, recipient, channel, message_preview, status

**Setup — two things to configure:**
① Set **n8n Variables** (project → Variables tab): `OPS1_SHEET_ID` (your Google Sheet ID) and `FIRM_NAME` (your firm name)
② Wire a **Google Sheets** credential (OAuth2) in the project → Credentials tab

**Caller integration (Path A) — add this node to any template before its send step:**
```
Node type: Execute Sub-workflow
Workflow: (select Bar-Compliance Guardrail)
Mode: Run once for each item
Inputs: channel, recipient, message, template_id, matter_id
```
See deploy guide for the exact copy-paste config and the approved/suppressed IF gate.

nodes.name: Receive Compliance Request
nodes.notes:
Entry point for parent templates — receives the compliance check request.

Do not activate this workflow standalone. It runs only when called via an 'Execute Sub-workflow' node in a parent template.

Expected input from the calling template:
  channel — 'sms' or 'email'
  recipient — phone (E.164 +1XXXXXXXXXX) for SMS; email address for email
  message — the planned outbound message text
  template_id — which template is calling, e.g. 'NTC-17' (for your audit log)
  matter_id — optional matter reference for context

firm_name and sheet_id are set internally in Configure Guardrail — callers do not need to pass them.

nodes.name: Configure Guardrail
nodes.notes:
Reads shared firm config from n8n Variables (project → Variables tab). Set once; all templates benefit automatically.

Variables to set:
  OPS1_SHEET_ID — the ID from your Google Sheet URL:
    https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SHEET_ID/edit
    Create the sheet first: New Google Sheet → rename to 'OPS1 Compliance Log' → add two tabs named exactly 'Opt-Outs' and 'Audit Log' → add the column headers listed in the sticky note.
  FIRM_NAME — your law firm name as it appears in outbound email footers.

All calling templates share these variables — if you move the sheet or rename the firm, update the n8n Variable once and every template picks it up.

nodes.name: Validate & Prepare
nodes.notes:
Validates that the calling template passed all required fields and normalises them for downstream nodes.

Required: channel (sms|email), recipient, message, template_id.
Optional: matter_id.

Throws a descriptive error if a field is missing — names the calling template so the problem is easy to locate. A silent failure here would send a non-compliant message with no audit trail.

nodes.name: Read Opt-Out List
nodes.notes:
Reads all rows from the Opt-Outs tab. The next step filters for the specific recipient.

Credential: project → Credentials tab → New → search 'Google Sheets OAuth2' → sign in with the Google account that owns the compliance sheet → save as 'Google Sheets account'.

If the Opt-Outs tab is empty (no one has opted out yet), this returns 0 rows. The next step handles that safely — 0 rows means approved.

The Opt-Outs tab must have these column headers in row 1: phone | email | opted_out_at | source

Add opt-outs manually, or they are added automatically when clients text STOP (Path B of this workflow).

nodes.name: Check Opt-Out Status
nodes.notes:
Checks whether the recipient is in the Opt-Outs list.

Matches phone numbers (E.164 format) for SMS and email addresses for email — both compared case-insensitively.

Runs even when the opt-out list is empty (0 rows) — no entries means the recipient is not opted out and the message is approved.

Output: is_opted_out (true/false) plus all fields from Validate & Prepare forwarded.

nodes.name: Opted Out?
nodes.notes:
Routes the request based on opt-out status.

Yes (opted out) → log the suppression and return { approved: false } to the calling template. No message is sent.
No (not opted out) → proceed to disclaimer attachment and audit logging.

nodes.name: Log Suppression
nodes.notes:
Appends a row to the Audit Log tab recording that this message was suppressed due to opt-out.

Logged fields: timestamp, template_id, matter_id, recipient, channel, first 200 chars of the planned message, status = 'suppressed'.

If the Google Sheets write fails, the workflow still returns { approved: false } to the caller — the suppression stands even if the log write fails. Review failed executions in n8n to catch any logging gaps.

nodes.name: Result — Suppressed
nodes.notes:
Returns the compliance result to the calling template.

Output: all original caller fields forwarded (recipient, matter_id, etc.) plus:
  approved: false
  message_out: '' (empty — do not send)
  suppression_reason: 'opted_out'

The calling template must check $json.approved before its send step. If false, route to a Skip node — do not send the message.

nodes.name: Attach Disclaimer
nodes.notes:
Appends the TCPA-required opt-out instruction to every approved outbound message.

SMS: appends 'Reply STOP to opt out.' on a new line — skipped if the message already contains a STOP instruction.
Email: appends a plain-text unsubscribe footer with the firm name — skipped if already present.

The calling template must use message_out (not its original message) for the Twilio or emailSend node — this is the version that carries the disclaimer.

nodes.name: Log to Audit
nodes.notes:
Appends a row to the Audit Log tab for every approved outbound message.

Logged fields: timestamp, template_id, matter_id, recipient, channel, first 200 chars of the message with disclaimer, status = 'approved'.

If the Google Sheets write fails (e.g., network error), the workflow still returns { approved: true } — a logging failure does not block your client message. Review failed executions to catch any gaps.

nodes.name: Result — Approved
nodes.notes:
Returns the compliance result to the calling template.

Output: all original caller fields forwarded (recipient, matter_id, etc.) plus:
  approved: true
  message_out: '<original message with STOP/unsubscribe disclaimer appended>'
  suppression_reason: null

The calling template must:
1. Check $json.approved — route to Skip if false
2. Use $json.message_out for the Twilio / emailSend node — this version carries the required disclaimer

nodes.name: Receive STOP Reply
nodes.notes:
Receives every inbound SMS from your Twilio number.

Activate this workflow so STOP replies land here automatically. Sub-workflow calls from parent templates (NTC-14, NTC-17, etc.) continue to work whether or not this workflow is active.

Twilio setup:
  Twilio Console → Phone Numbers → Manage → Active Numbers → click your number → Messaging Configuration → 'When a message comes in' → Webhook → paste the Production URL from this node → HTTP POST → Save.

Important: only one URL can receive inbound SMS per Twilio number. If you already have a reply webhook (e.g., NTC-17 review reply on the same number), pointing Twilio here will stop NTC-17 from receiving those replies. Use a separate Twilio number for review requests, or consolidate inbound routing into a single entry-point workflow.

Twilio POSTs: From (client E.164 number), To (firm number), Body (message text), MessageSid, NumMedia.

n8n auto-returns HTTP 200 to Twilio — no TwiML response node needed.

nodes.name: Read Inbound SMS
nodes.notes:
Parses the Twilio inbound SMS and classifies it.

Keyword matching follows TCPA and Twilio's standard opt-out word lists:
  STOP keywords: STOP, UNSUBSCRIBE, CANCEL, END, QUIT
  UNSTOP keywords: UNSTOP, START

Matching is case-insensitive and anchored to the first word of the message body so 'Stop please' and 'STOP' both match. Extra whitespace is trimmed.

Also loads OPS1_SHEET_ID from n8n Variables — the same sheet used by the sub-workflow path. Set it once in project → Variables tab.

Output: from_phone (E.164), sms_body, is_stop (bool), is_unstop (bool), sheet_id, received_at.

nodes.name: STOP keyword?
nodes.notes:
Routes STOP keywords to the opt-out append path.

Yes (STOP/UNSUBSCRIBE/CANCEL/END/QUIT) → read the Opt-Outs tab to check for a duplicate before appending.
No → check for UNSTOP keyword on the next gate.

nodes.name: Read Opt-Outs for STOP
nodes.notes:
Reads the current Opt-Outs tab before appending — used to detect duplicate STOP entries.

If this phone number is already on the list, the workflow routes to a Skip node rather than appending a duplicate. This prevents the sheet from accumulating repeated entries for the same number if a client sends STOP more than once.

If the Opt-Outs tab is empty (no one has opted out yet), this returns 0 rows — the duplicate-check node handles that safely.

Credential: same Google Sheets account used throughout this workflow.

nodes.name: Already opted out?
nodes.notes:
Checks whether this phone number is already in the Opt-Outs tab.

Phone numbers are compared case-insensitively after trimming. Twilio sends E.164 format (+14155551234) — if attorneys have entered numbers manually in a different format, the comparison may not match. Keep phone entries in E.164 format to ensure correct matching.

If the opt-out tab is empty (no rows yet), this safely returns already_opted_out: false.

Output: from_phone, sheet_id, received_at, already_opted_out (bool).

nodes.name: Duplicate STOP?
nodes.notes:
Prevents duplicate opt-out entries.

Yes (already on the list) → Skip — already opted out. No second entry written.
No (not on the list) → Append Opt-Out Entry.

nodes.name: Skip — already opted out
nodes.notes: Phone number is already in the Opt-Outs tab — no duplicate entry written. Twilio still receives HTTP 200. Visible in execution history for auditing.

nodes.name: Append Opt-Out Entry
nodes.notes:
Appends a new row to the Opt-Outs tab when a STOP keyword is received.

Columns written:
  phone — client's E.164 phone number from Twilio's From field
  email — left blank (STOP arrives by SMS; email opt-out must be managed separately)
  opted_out_at — ISO 8601 timestamp of when the STOP was received
  source — 'twilio-stop' so you can distinguish automated entries from manually added ones

The sub-workflow path reads this tab on every outbound message — once this row is written, all future messages to this number are automatically suppressed.

If the write fails (e.g., Google API error), the workflow still returns HTTP 200 to Twilio. Review failed executions in n8n to catch any logging gaps.

nodes.name: UNSTOP keyword?
nodes.notes:
Routes UNSTOP/START keywords to the opt-out removal path.

Yes (UNSTOP or START) → read the Opt-Outs tab to find and remove this phone number.
No (unrelated inbound SMS — e.g., a question or general reply) → Skip — not a STOP or UNSTOP reply.

nodes.name: Read Opt-Outs for UNSTOP
nodes.notes:
Reads all rows from the Opt-Outs tab to find the matching phone number for removal.

The next node (Find Row to Remove) scans the returned rows for the UNSTOP sender's phone number and extracts its row_number — the sheet row index that the delete node needs.

If the tab is empty (no opted-out numbers), this returns 0 rows. The next node handles that safely and routes to Skip — not on opt-out list.

Credential: same Google Sheets account used throughout this workflow.

nodes.name: Find Row to Remove
nodes.notes:
Scans the Opt-Outs tab rows and extracts the row_number of the matching phone entry.

row_number is provided by the Google Sheets read node on every output row. It equals the actual spreadsheet row number (1 = header, 2 = first data row). The delete node's 'startIndex' parameter accepts this value directly.

Phone numbers are matched case-insensitively. Non-E.164 entries added manually (e.g., '415-555-1234' vs '+14155551234') will not match an E.164 UNSTOP sender — in that case the row is not found and the Skip branch runs. The attorney can remove the entry manually if needed.

Output: from_phone, sheet_id, found_row_number (integer or null), row_found (bool).

nodes.name: Row found?
nodes.notes:
Routes based on whether the phone number is currently in the Opt-Outs tab.

Yes (found) → Delete Opt-Out Row.
No (not found) → Skip — not on opt-out list. Twilio still receives HTTP 200. The local list simply had no entry for this number — Twilio's carrier-level re-subscription is already handled by Twilio itself.

nodes.name: Delete Opt-Out Row
nodes.notes:
Removes the client's phone number from the Opt-Outs tab when they send UNSTOP or START.

Uses found_row_number from Find Row to Remove — this is the actual spreadsheet row number (1-based, header = 1) returned by the Google Sheets read node. The delete node's 'startIndex' parameter accepts this value directly.

After this row is deleted, future outbound messages to this number are no longer blocked by the sub-workflow opt-out check.

Note: Twilio also processes UNSTOP at the carrier level independently. This node clears only the local Google Sheet opt-out list.

nodes.name: Skip — not on opt-out list
nodes.notes: Phone number sent UNSTOP but was not found in the Opt-Outs tab — no sheet change needed. Twilio still receives HTTP 200. Twilio's carrier-level re-subscription is already processed by Twilio independently of this sheet.

nodes.name: Skip — not a STOP or UNSTOP reply
nodes.notes: Inbound message is not a STOP or UNSTOP keyword — no action taken. This branch handles all other inbound SMS on your Twilio number (questions, general replies, etc.). Twilio receives HTTP 200.

json.message: Hi Jane, it was our honor to serve you at Smith Law Firm. How was your experience? Reply 1–5 (1 = poor, 5 = excellent).

main.node: Skip — not a STOP or UNSTOP reply
